heading: MongoDB Vector Search Scoring
body:
MongoDB Vector Search assigns a score, in a fixed range from `0` to `1`
(where `0` indicates low similarity and `1` indicates high
similarity), to every document that it returns.

The score is calculated according to the `similarity` function that
you specify in the MongoDB Vector Search [index definition](avs-types-vector-search-options). To learn more about the
`similarity` options you can choose from, see
[About the Similarity Functions](avs-similarity-functions).

The score is calculated according to the `similarity` function that
you specify in the MongoDB Vector Search [index definition](avs-types-vector-search-options). To learn more about the
`similarity` options you can choose from, see
[About the Similarity Functions](avs-similarity-functions).

Each returned document includes the score as metadata. To return each
document's score along with the result set, use a
[$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage in your aggregation pipeline and configure
the `score` as a field to project. In the `score` field, specify a
[$meta](manual/reference/mql/expressions) expression
with the value `vectorSearchScore`. The syntax is as follows:

```javascript
db.<collection>.aggregate([
  {
    "$vectorSearch": {
      <query-syntax>
    }
  },
  {
    "$project": {
      "<field-to-include>": 1,
      "<field-to-exclude>": 0,
      "score": { "$meta": "vectorSearchScore" }
    }
  }
])
```

> **Note:** You can use `vectorSearchScore` as a `score` [$meta](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/meta) expression only after the
> [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) pipeline stage. If you use
> `vectorSearchScore` after any other query, MongoDB logs a warning
> starting in MongoDB v8.2.

> **Note:** Pre-filtering your data doesn't affect the score that MongoDB Vector Search returns
> using `vectorSearchScore` for [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) queries.
